data.title: Ukraine and U.S. Progress in Mineral Resource Agreement Talks
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has announced positive developments in discussions with the United States regarding a mineral resource agreement, with plans for further meetings in the future.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that on March 28, a proposal draft summary revealed that the Trump administration had introduced a new, broader mineral agreement with Ukraine. Sources indicate that the U.S. has revised its initial proposal, opting not to provide future security guarantees to Ukraine but requiring it to contribute all revenue from the use of natural resources managed by state-owned and private enterprises within Ukraine to a joint investment fund.

The terms proposed by Washington significantly exceed those discussed in the controversial meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Zelensky in the Oval Office last month. One source mentioned that U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent has been leading the negotiations on behalf of the United States. Additionally, the summary does not address the issue of U.S. ownership of Ukrainian nuclear power plants, a topic previously mentioned by Trump.

data.title: Bitdeer Plans U.S. Manufacturing Expansion Amid Global Growth
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer is set to capitalize on a 90-day tariff suspension period to transport mining equipment from Southeast Asia to the United States. The company aims to launch a manufacturing facility in the U.S. later this year, focusing on producing mining machines for its own operations rather than selling them to other miners.

Bitdeer is also expanding its global presence by entering new markets such as Canada and Ethiopia. Additionally, the company is advancing its strategic shift towards artificial intelligence and high-performance computing sectors.

data.title: DeFi startup Treehouse Finance completes new round of financing with a valuation of $400 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News DeFi startup Treehouse Finance has completed a new round of financing with a valuation of US$400 million. The specific financing and investor information has not been disclosed, but MassMutual Ventures is said to have participated in the investment. The company's co-founder and CEO Brandon Goh said that this round of financing uses the Simple Agreement for Future Tokens (SAFT), and the company's previous US$18 million seed round of financing also used the SAFT structure. (The Block)

data.title: Multicoin executive partner borrowed 7 million USDC and deposited it in Coinbase, suspected to increase SOL holdings
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, Multicoin Cap Managing Partner Kyle Samani borrowed 7 million USDC from Kamino and deposited it into Coinbase 2 hours ago, and is suspected of increasing his holdings of SOL.

data.title: Trump's latest crypto project will be a real estate crypto game
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to two familiar sources, Trump's latest project will be a Trump-branded crypto game, which is also a game with a Monopoly-like game experience. It is reported that this game may be a variant of Monopoly GO! Players earn game coins by moving chess pieces on a digital Monopoly board to build buildings in a digital city. Another anonymous source familiar with the game also compared the project to Monopoly. Both informed sources requested anonymity. (Fortune)

data.title: US policy on stablecoins is way more dangerous than tariffs – Italy’s Economy Minister
data.description:
Italy’s economy minister, Giancarlo Giorgetti, says US policy on stablecoins is a bigger threat to European economic sovereignty than trade tariffs. Speaking at an asset management event in Milan on Tuesday, he reiterated that the global influence of dollar-denominated stablecoins undermines the European Union’s (EU) monetary independence. President Donald Trump has made policy changes to […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-stablecoins-policy-dangerous-than-tariffs/

data.title: US anti-inflation activists launch USDDi stablecoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Two veterans in the field of inflation protection and foreign exchange derivatives in the United States have launched the US dollar stablecoin USDi, whose value is determined by the growth of the US Consumer Price Index (CPI) since December 2024. As of April 15, its value was $1.00863. According to Michael Ashton, who started his inflation-proof investment career at Barclays Bank in the early 2000s, USDi is equivalent to the principal of TIPS, or in theory, similar to an inflation-proof savings account. According to a statement from USDi Partners LLC, the token will have the same purchasing power as the US dollar in December 2024. USDi will mint and destroy tokens at their marked value, which, like the principal of TIPS, will depend on the CPI of the day. (Bloomberg)

data.title: There have been multiple large BTC withdrawals in the past 48 hours, with 4 whales withdrawing a total of 5,532 BTC
data.description:
According to Odaily Planet Daily, according to The Data Nerd, there have been multiple large BTC withdrawals in the past 48 hours, and 4 whales have withdrawn a total of 5,532 BTC, including: 
1. bc1qn withdrew 2,500 BTC (about 212.98 million US dollars) from OKX
2. bc1qq withdrew 200 BTC (about 17.11 million US dollars) from Binance
3. 168Bv (Cumberland) withdrew 460 BTC (about 39 million US dollars) from Binance
4. bc1qp (currently owns 16,780 BTC) withdrew 2,372 BTC (about 201.6 million US dollars) from Binance and Kraken

data.title: ZK briefly fell below 0.04 USDT, with a 24-hour drop of 10.45%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: OKX market data shows that ZK briefly fell below 0.04 USDT and is currently trading at 0.04576 USDT, with a 24-hour drop of 10.45%.

data.title: ZKsync: A hacked admin account stole about $5 million worth of ZK tokens
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: ZKsync announced that the ZKsync security team has confirmed that a hacked administrator account has stolen approximately $5 million worth of ZK tokens (i.e. the remaining unclaimed tokens in the ZKsync airdrop), and is currently taking necessary security measures. All user funds are safe and have never been at risk. The ZKsync protocol and ZK token contracts are safe, and ZK is not at risk.
